authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC Franchise Rule is the federal baseline for franchise disclosure records, required fee disclosures, disclosure versions, signed receipt retention, annual updates, and material-change updates that anchor FDD fee evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Franchise fee source validation, FDD fee context, receipt retention, disclosure-version control, and legal-boundary routing
used_for: FDD and fee schedule source hierarchy
franchise-specific record retention and disclosure-version expectations
boundary between administrative records and franchise legal compliance
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires royalty administration outputs to cite franchise agreement and FDD fee context instead of relying on informal fee history or generic billing records.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC business guidance explains franchise disclosure timing, fee disclosure, receipt retention, electronic delivery, and financial performance representation boundaries used to keep royalty administration tied to proper franchise records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Disclosure record context, fee-term evidence review, and boundary handling
used_for: official FTC terminology for franchise disclosure records
records needed before fee-related administrative outputs can be treated as complete
routing of legal, disclosure, and earnings-claim issues outside the lane
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating royalty records as ordinary receivables disconnected from franchise disclosure controls.

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual: 533110 Lessors of Nonfinancial Intangible Assets (except Copyrighted Works)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau publishes NAICS and identifies franchise agreements, royalty payments, and licensing fees paid to asset holders, making it authoritative for connecting the lane to franchise agreement and intangible-rights records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry boundary and royalty terminology validation
used_for: franchise agreement and royalty payment terminology
distinction between franchise/intangible-rights royalty records and generic administrative records
industry-specific artifact expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors the package to royalty payments and franchise agreements so the resolved donor/member template cannot dominate the final lane.

authoritative_sources.title: 2008 Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA guidelines are the recognized registration-state franchise disclosure workflow reference and define FDD item structure, fee schedules, exhibits, receipts, and state filing evidence relevant to royalty records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FDD fee schedule, state addendum, exhibit, and receipt evidence review
used_for: Item 6 and other fee evidence context
registration-state and state addendum awareness
document packet completeness and stale-source escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains royalty package review to current, jurisdiction-aware franchise disclosure records and avoids assuming one fee schedule applies across states or brands.

authoritative_sources.title: NASAA Franchise Multi Unit Commentary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's franchise resource library identifies the adopted multi-unit commentary used by registration-state reviewers to clarify area development, subfranchise, and area representative structures that change royalty participants, splits, records, and authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Multi-unit participant, territory, area-representative, and subfranchisor context validation
used_for: multi-unit franchisee and area representative participant context
subfranchisor and area development evidence requirements
routing when royalty split or territory authority is not administrative
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces multi-unit royalty summaries to capture participant, territory, unit, and role context rather than flattening all payments into one franchisor receivable.

authoritative_sources.title: ASU 2021-02, Franchisors - Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Subtopic 952-606)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FASB's franchisor-specific revenue guidance identifies franchise agreements, initial franchise fees, sales-based royalties, and revenue-recognition boundaries that require accounting-owner review rather than administrative improvisation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accounting-policy handoff and revenue-recognition boundary control
used_for: sales-based royalty and franchise agreement accounting terminology
distinction between evidence preparation and accounting policy decisions
escalation of revenue-recognition or GAAP questions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from deciding revenue recognition, initial franchise fee treatment, or accounting policy while still preparing the evidence that accounting owners need.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 525, Taxable and Nontaxable Income
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS guidance identifies royalties as taxable income and provides tax-reporting context, constraining royalty administration to evidence preparation and tax-owner routing rather than tax advice or filing decisions.
used_for: royalty tax terminology
tax-owner handoff triggers
refusal of tax advice or filing-position decisions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires tax-sensitive royalty questions to be routed while the specialist limits work to records, evidence, and administrative status.

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records-management authority, and these requirements define record metadata, file plans, retention, disposition, access, retrieval, integrity, and audit expectations for electronic record packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record metadata, retention, access, retrieval, audit, and disposition control
used_for: record classification and metadata expectations
retention and disposition boundaries
access and audit-trail requirements
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to governed record maintenance and escalates retention, access, legal hold, and disposition questions.

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control catalog for access control, audit and accountability, PII processing, and system integrity controls relevant to franchisee financial and payment records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access, privacy, audit, payment-data, and system-control verification
used_for: authorized-user evidence
audit and accountability expectations
privacy and payment-data handling escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires access, repository permission, audit-log, and privacy-sensitive handling evidence before distribution, update, publication, or handoff.

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Franchise agreement, amendments, FDD Item 6 fee schedule, state addenda, unit roster, POS exports, franchisor portal sales reports, gross-sales detail, invoice/AR records, ACH or card settlement, bank deposits, approved credits or abatements, dispute cases, audit adjustments, document repository, franchise management system, ERP/GL/AR, payment/bank systems, workflow queue, IAM, and audit logs govern the lane.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Royalty administration fails materially when the lane ignores franchise agreement terms, stale or conflicting FDD fee schedules, unit-specific gross-sales definitions, multi-unit area-representative splits, sales-report and payment matching gaps, franchisee financial data access controls, or legal/accounting/tax authority boundaries.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Legal interpretation, FDD compliance, default/termination notices, GAAP revenue recognition, tax filing positions, AR collection strategy, audit finding approval, payment authorization, write-off or waiver approval, privacy/IAM decisions, retention-disposition approval, and executive signoff remain outside the lane.

workflow_stages.name: Royalty request intake and authority validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate brand, unit, franchisee, period, fee type, jurisdiction, franchisor/subfranchisor/area-representative role, tenant SOP, approval matrix, source hierarchy, requester authority, and requested outcome before work begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Franchise fee source-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect current franchise agreement, amendment, addendum, FDD Item 6 fee schedule, state addenda, approved exception, credit, abatement, waiver, or audit adjustment from authoritative repositories.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Unit sales and payment evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Validate unit roster, POS exports, portal sales reports, gross-sales detail, excluded-sales support, invoices, AR ledger, ACH or card settlement, bank deposits, returned payments, reconciliation reports, disputes, and collection workflow records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Delegated status and metadata maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Update only permitted received status, due-date status, evidence checklist, exception code, payment matching status, dispute flag, owner, and audit-note fields tied to source evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, accounting, tax, and governance control review
workflow_stages.description: Check retention category, legal hold, repository permissions, privacy/payment-data handling, accounting handoff, tax handoff, approval ownership, and audit trail before distribution, completion, or handoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, handoff, and traceability closure
workflow_stages.description: Hold, rework, route, or complete packages when evidence is incomplete, source records conflict, authority shifts, or tenant adaptation is required; produce source-linked summaries and escalation packets.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Tenant service model, brand or concept, unit or territory, franchisee master, role model for franchisor/subfranchisor/area representative, SLA, SOP, approval matrix, and source hierarchy

Franchise agreement, amendment, addendum, area development agreement, area representative agreement, FDD version, Item 6 fee schedule, state addenda, approved fee exception, credit memo, abatement, waiver, and audit adjustment

Unit roster, opening and closure dates, ownership or transfer records, period, fee type, POS sales report, portal submission, gross-sales detail, excluded-sales support, local ad support, and late or missing report notice
Invoice, debit memo, AR ledger, payment authorization, ACH batch, card settlement, lockbox record, bank deposit, returned payment, reconciliation report, collection workflow item, and dispute case
Franchise management platform, POS, franchisor portal, ERP, GL, AR, billing, payment processor, bank, document repository, workflow queue, CRM, IAM, secure communication, and audit-log records

Retention schedule, legal-hold flag, access entitlement, repository permission, data classification, redaction marker, QA checklist, exception code, missing-item owner and due date, record update note, and audit-trail summary
systems_of_record: Franchise management platform and franchisee master
Franchise agreement, FDD, amendment, addenda, and disclosure document repository
POS, sales reporting, franchisor portal, and unit reporting systems
ERP, GL, billing, AR, and revenue subledger systems
Payment processor, ACH, card settlement, lockbox, bank, and reconciliation systems
Workflow queue, collection/dispute case system, and exception tracker
Document management, records management, retention schedule, legal-hold, and secure file-transfer systems
Identity and access management, repository permission, quality review, and audit-log systems

allowed: Determine whether available franchise agreement, FDD fee schedule, unit, sales-report, payment, adjustment, retention, access, QA, and audit evidence support blocked, incomplete, ready-for-review, ready-for-handoff, exceptioned, or completed-with-evidence status.

Update only delegated royalty package metadata, received status, attachment checklist, due-date status, payment-match status, dispute flag, owner, exception code, and audit-note fields inside tenant systems of record.

Produce royalty records completeness summaries, administrative status notes, retention/access/governance escalation packages, franchise royalty workflow summaries, evidence packets, and audit-trail summaries tied to named records.
escalate: Franchise agreement, FDD, state addendum, tenant policy, POS report, portal report, AR ledger, payment record, bank record, accounting instruction, or tax instruction materially conflicts.
Retention, legal-hold, access, privacy, redaction, publication, distribution, disposal, payment-data, or repository-permission rules are missing or conflict with the request.

The request requires legal, franchise compliance, accounting policy, tax, audit, AR collections, payment approval, fee waiver, write-off, privacy, IAM, records-disposition, policy-override, official-record, or executive approval authority.
refuse: The request asks the lane to bypass missing prerequisites, source-record conflicts, approvals, access controls, QA failures, retention rules, legal holds, or payment evidence gaps.
The request asks to decide the underlying royalty dispute, legal/default status, tax treatment, accounting treatment, audit finding, write-off, waiver, or financial approval.
Required tenant facts, authoritative source context, systems of record, record locations, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the outcome.
The request replaces franchise royalty artifacts with generic contribution, donor, member, dues, or administrative-processing abstractions.

domain_failure_modes: Accepting a royalty package without current franchise agreement, FDD Item 6 fee schedule, approved exceptions, unit roster, period, fee type, or tenant source hierarchy.
Treating email, chat, spreadsheets, or verbal assurances as substitutes for authoritative franchise management, POS, portal, ERP, AR, payment, bank, document, IAM, workflow, or audit systems.
Changing royalty rate, gross-sales exclusion, late fee, credit, waiver, unit status, area-representative split, or period status without source evidence and delegated approval.
Choosing one source when the FDD, franchise agreement, portal fee schedule, AR ledger, POS report, payment processor, bank, or tenant SOP conflict materially.
Distributing franchisee sales reports, ACH details, disputes, or payment data while retention, legal-hold, privacy, access, redaction, or repository-permission rules are unresolved.

Collapsing royalty administration into generic billing, donor contribution, member dues, nonprofit participant, finance approval, contract interpretation, legal, tax, accounting, AR collections, audit, privacy, IAM, or records-governance ownership.
Flattening tenant and brand differences in fee schedules, gross-sales definitions, POS systems, ad-fund rules, area-representative splits, jurisdictions, retention classes, QA rules, or access rules.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State franchise registration and relationship-law details
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires jurisdiction, state addenda, agreement terms, and legal-owner routing before acting where state-specific rules could change the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific gross-sales definitions and fee exceptions
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires current agreement terms, approved exceptions, and source hierarchy; missing or conflicting terms block administrative completion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Revenue-recognition, tax, collections, audit, and write-off authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack limits the specialist to evidence preparation, status maintenance, and routing; adjacent owners make substantive accounting, tax, collections, audit, and approval decisions.

market_ready_research_conclusion: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because jurisdiction, agreement, fee, source-system, accounting, tax, retention, access, and approval variance is converted into explicit retrieval dependencies, refusal rules, and adjacent-lane escalation.
